profile.name: Zane Davis
profile.tagline: I build robots that obey. Sometimes me.
profile.bio: I’m Zane, a high schooler obsessed with code, robots, and making machines do my bidding. I spend most of my time building AI, automating systems, and tinkering with projects that probably shouldn’t exist… yet. Always iterating, always optimizing, always thinking of new solutions to even the utmost simple of problems (and spending too much time on them as well).
profile.location: Silverton, Oregon, United States of America

github.statsNote:
Open source fan. Shipping, learning, and sharing in public. Feel free to check out my repos- almost everything is free to use.
If you really like my work, please consider buying me a coffee!

projects.title: SwipeClean
projects.description: Really small but useful python script I made! Makes it really easy to clean out large folders, without having to use the overwhelming Windows explorer UX.

projects.title: Easy-Custom-Titlebar
projects.description: A python package that makes it really easy to have a custom titlebar, like the ones fancy apps have these days. Instead of having the ugly white title bar that Windows natively comes with, it hides it completely, and communicates directly to system APIs. Can break easily in future system updates, if that happens, feel free to fork it or create an issue!

experience.description: just doin whatever i think is cool
I am the lead programmer for the FRC team Hotwire Robotics (2990!). I mostly focus on logging and other adjacent things. I just like hopping around and helping/building whatever I find!
